hypotheses.description: Enable time-of-day filter to cut Asian/off-hours trades. hour_cos is the 2nd most important feature (0.7311 SHAP) and its interaction with direction (0.1227) is the strongest pairwise signal. Decoding: hour_cos >= 0.63 maps to ~20:30-03:30 UTC (Asian/late-NY), where the weak filter shows shorts hit only 11.1% win rate (0.67x baseline). The time_of_day edge filter exists but is currently DISABLED for SSS. Enabling it with a London-open-through-mid-NY window removes the lowest-quality time zones while preserving the sessions where gold has structural liquidity and trending behavior.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: Removes ~25-30% of trades occurring in low-win-rate off-hours windows. Based on the weak filter evidence, these cut trades average 8-11% win rate vs 16.7% baseline. Remaining trades should see win rate lift to ~19-22%. Fewer trades but materially better expectancy.

hypotheses.description: Raise min_confluence_score from 2 to 3. confluence_score has 0.6643 mean |SHAP| â€” 3rd most important predictor. The current threshold of 2 on a 0-8 scale is extremely permissive (only requires 1 of 6 confluence components beyond minimum). The SSS confluence score sums: SS alignment (+2), ISS alignment (+2), CBC confirmation (+1), fifty-tap (+1), target proximity (+1), no F2 conflict (+1). A score of 2 means essentially zero alignment beyond the entry trigger. Raising to 3 requires at least one structural alignment component (e.g., SS direction matching sequence direction), filtering out trades with no contextual support.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: Filters out the bottom tier of low-conviction setups. Given confluence_score's high SHAP importance, trades with score=2 are likely dragging down overall performance. Expected win rate improvement of 2-4 percentage points on retained trades. Also reduces overtrading, which helps with prop firm daily loss limits.

hypotheses.description: Avoid longs during London/NY session. The weakest filter discovered shows hour_cos < 0.63 AND direction >= 0.50 (London/NY longs) at only 8.3% win rate â€” half the baseline (0.50x lift, n=12). Combined with the strong hour_cos Ã— direction interaction (0.1227 SHAP), this suggests a structural session-direction bias: gold tends to sell off during London/NY in this sample window, making long entries during 03:30-20:30 UTC systematically unfavorable. This can be implemented by adding a direction-aware session filter as a new edge, or as a first approximation, by narrowing the allowed trading window to the overlap where both directions work (roughly 18:00-03:30 UTC for longs, keeping London/NY open for shorts only). However, since no existing config param directly encodes directionÃ—session, the most practical proxy is to raise the confluence bar specifically for the permissive direction.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: By requiring score >= 4 (at least 2 structural alignment components), we indirectly filter out the low-quality directional trades that lack multi-timeframe confirmation. The 12 long trades during London/NY with 8.3% win rate likely have low confluence. This is a blunt proxy â€” a proper directionÃ—session filter would be more surgical, but this is testable immediately with existing config. Expected to cut trade count by ~30-40% while lifting win rate by 3-5pp.
